items.caption: Fourth JCC Meeting, 8 June 2026 at JST Annex, Tokyo — SATREPS partners from UTeM, UNITEN, and Kindai University reviewed project progress and signed a renewed commitment to the collaboration.
ICLP 2026, Sapporo (31 May–5 June) — ten project papers from Kindai University, UTeM, and UNITEN shared the latest RTL3D findings with the international lightning community.
14–15 May field work — DID Ampang site survey, maintenance at TM Bukit Gasing, and a noise measurement test at UiTM Shah Alam ahead of new sensor deployment in the Klang Valley.
Last day of the Melaka RTL campaign, 9 May 2026 — training at UiTM Jasin and UTeM concluded after a successful lightning trigger on 3 May, marking Malaysia's first RTL field programme.
3 May 2026 — first successful rocket-triggered lightning in Malaysia at the UTeM RTL site, a historic milestone for the RTL3D programme and lightning research in the country.
RTL campaign Part 1, 27 April 2026, UiTM Jasin — preparations and a successful first test launch of a new rocket from UiTM Shah Alam ahead of Malaysia's inaugural RTL programme.
Kanazawa RTL campaign, Dec 2025–Mar 2026 — a record seven successful rocket-triggered lightning strikes, paving the way for the world's lowest-latitude RTL experiment in Malaysia.
TM Bukit Gasing inspection — Dr. Takeshi Kudo checked Rogowski coil data loggers to verify current sensors are operating correctly and recording accurate lightning measurements.

Gamma-ray and radon sensors from Japan installed at three UNITEN sites — monitoring thunderstorm radiation and atmospheric radon, with installation know-how shared by Prof. Torii, Mr. Miyazaki, and Dr. Zafri.
North of Kuala Lumpur — Dr. Takayanagi and Dr. Haziq from Kindai University install a VHF sensor with UNITEN and DID support, extending the RTL3D lightning observation network.
Jasin, Melaka — Dr. Takayanagi and Dr. Haziq from Kindai University install a new VHF sensor with UTeM and UiTM Jasin, recording the site's first observation data.
12–13 March 2025, TM Bukit Gasing — five Rogowski coils installed around a communications tower to measure lightning current, with Malaysian and Japanese teams completing setup and inspection.
UiTM Jasin, 26 February — LF sensor and electric field mill installed to monitor lightning in Melaka, identified as a future rocket-triggered lightning research site.
20 December, Kanazawa RTL site — UNITEN and MET Malaysia teams arrive for rocket-triggered lightning training, with a briefing on earlier December field sessions.
